flags.concern: Shot positioning/order: the plan says new shots can be populated as unpositioned and even suggests a Promise.all fallback for batch insert, but the repository's video-generation flow only treats positioned images as valid input. `runAddImageMutation()` maps `timelineFrame: null` to an unpositioned insert, and `useVideoGenerationModalController` filters to positioned images then blocks generation when none exist. On the timeline side, `selectedClipIds` is an insertion-ordered Set, so Step 7 also needs an explicit sort by clip `at`/track order before assigning frames or the created shot will not preserve timeline order.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/shots/addImageToShotHelpers.ts` treats `timelineFrame === null` as unpositioned. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/travel-between-images/components/hooks/useVideoGenerationModalController.ts` builds `positionedImages` from positioned shot entries and errors with 'At least 1 positioned image is required.' `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMultiSelect.ts` stores selected clip ids in a Set with insertion order, not timeline order.

flags.concern: Shot naming: Step 2 invents a new default like 'Shot from N items', but the repository already centralizes auto-naming in `useShotCreation()` as `Shot ${count + 1}` and the user explicitly approved that convention. A parallel naming rule in a new hook will diverge from the rest of the app.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/useShotCreation.ts` defines `generateShotName()` as `Shot ${count + 1}`. Existing gallery and shot-list creation flows use `useShotCreation`, including `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useGenerationsPaneController.ts` and `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/travel-between-images/pages/ShotListView.tsx`.

flags.concern: Cross-surface selection: the plan explicitly narrows 'across both' to 'whichever system has a selection', but the repository currently enforces mutual exclusion between gallery and timeline selection. If the product requirement still means combining selected gallery items and selected timeline clips into one shot or video action, this plan does not touch the selection model that prevents it.
flags.evidence: `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.ts` calls `clearGallerySelection()` on normal timeline selection and registers a peer clearer back into `GallerySelectionContext`. `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx` stores that peer clearer and invokes it on non-toggle gallery selection. The plan's assumption section then says there is no unified cross-context selection and only one system will be read.
